module.name: agent
module.purpose: Agent crate namespace for model/session/task/prompt/step/execution orchestration and the plan queue module. Step now owns task-step routing, a new execution module tracks tool executions keyed by execution signature, and task runtime maps execution signatures to step signatures so execution success can complete steps and trigger follow-up execution-analysis model plans.

childModules.name: execution
childModules.purpose: Owns agent-side execution tracking: Execution values keyed by ExecutionSignature and an ExecutionHub that owns an Arc<TaskState> and an execution map to route execution events and report per-signature status.

childModules.name: model
childModules.purpose: Owns agent model backend traits, request/response data, backend errors, and the implemented Deepseek streaming backend. Internal backend implementations expose request directly instead of a send adapter.

childModules.name: plan
childModules.purpose: Owns PlanQueue, PlanRecord, and PlanError for insertion-ordered task plan storage, lookup, completion tracking, and current/pending plan text rendering for prompts.

childModules.name: prompt
childModules.purpose: Defines the agent prompt interface, render_template rendering, InitialPrompt, and ExecutionAnalysisPrompt for execution-output analysis.

childModules.name: session
childModules.purpose: Owns Session startup/routing behavior, including forwarding Step events to clients and routing them back to the owning Task worker, SessionContext snapshots for prompts, and a no-op Plan event arm.

childModules.name: step
childModules.purpose: Owns the top-level agent runtime Step type re-exported by lib.rs; Step holds Arc<TaskState> and now owns task-step routing, plan execution, model-prompt rendering, and step/model completion handling.

childModules.name: task
childModules.purpose: Owns agent task runtime and TaskState (session context, plan queue, execution_map, and step queue); step routing now lives on Step while task_execution drives execution steps and execution-analysis follow-up plans.
